target.title: Import contact exports from source crawlers
target.body:
## Summary

- add the clawdex-owned `ContactExport` v0 proto artifact and strict JSON decoder
- add `clawdex import contacts --from <source crawler>`
- discover `contact-export` through crawlkit metadata, require `crawlkit.control.v1`, require JSON/read-only, bind execution to the same `--from` binary, and import only display name plus phone values into the existing `SourceContact` path
- align the proto package with the v0 contract: `openclaw.clawdex.contactexport.v0`
- move clawdex to Go 1.26.4 so CI `govulncheck` uses the fixed standard library

## Related PRs

- Consumer: https://github.com/openclaw/clawdex/pull/2
- WhatsApp producer: https://github.com/steipete/wacrawl/pull/12
- Telegram producer: https://github.com/openclaw/telecrawl/pull/9

These three PRs are one contact-export v0 slice. Source crawlers own source-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling the crawler metadata `contact-export` command. They should land in lockstep; if the command name, metadata argv, envelope, or field names change in one repo, all three should change together.

## Intent

The discoverable local `contact-export` metadata command is intentional. Clawdex discovering and running that read-only command is intentional. Local opt-in import of stored contact display names and phone numbers into clawdex is the feature boundary, not an accidental privacy expansion.

The v0 contract is deliberately small: `crawlkit.control.v1`, command name `contact-export`, JSON envelope `contacts`, fields `display_name` and `phone_numbers`, and read-only metadata argv. This PR intentionally does not add graph/candidate/source-id/JID/username/ranking fields or a generic crawler-to-crawler protocol.

The Go 1.26.4 bump is also intentional: `govulncheck` on Go 1.26.3 reports reachable standard-library findings fixed in Go 1.26.4. This does not need to be split unless maintainers want a separate release-policy change.

## Boundary

This is the clawdex consumer side of the same contact-import slice as `steipete/wacrawl#12` and `openclaw/telecrawl#9`. The crawler owns source-native contact extraction; clawdex owns canonical people and imports by pulling metadata-advertised `contact-export` commands.

This is intentionally a local CLI/control metadata surface, not a graph layer, candidate model, or broad crawler-to-crawler abstraction.

## Validation

- `nix shell nixpkgs#go_1_26 -c sh -lc 'GOWORK=off go test -count=1 ./... && GOWORK=off go test -count=1 -race ./... && GOWORK=off go mod verify && GOWORK=off go install golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@v1.3.0 && GOWORK=off "$(go env GOPATH)/bin/govulncheck" ./...'`
- `git diff --check`
- Go 1.26.4 check: `govulncheck` returns no vulnerabilities
- Go 1.26.3 comparison: `GOTOOLCHAIN=go1.26.3 govulncheck ./...` reports reachable standard-library findings `GO-2026-5039` and `GO-2026-5037`, both fixed in Go 1.26.4
- built-binary smoke against the telecrawl export command: aggregate dry-run actions only, `[{"action":"create","count":147},{"action":"update","count":53}]`
- adversarial review passes: accepted the schema-version guard and stricter decoder; rejected dependency/version churn because the inspected crawlkit control structs for v0.5.2 and v0.7.0 are identical for this surface

## Privacy

The payload shape is intentionally only `contacts[].display_name` and `contacts[].phone_numbers`. Smoke output was checked by aggregate counts/key shape only; no real contact names or phone numbers are included here.

target.author: joshp123
